mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation support by lowering discount rates and refinancing pressure, which raises S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is actively easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if triggered instances underperform baseline in more than 60% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in PAYEMS with a stable or falling UNRATE signals improving labor income and consumption capacity, which lifts near-term equity returns through better demand expectations over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE is flat or lower versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-flat-or-down condition does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the interaction term has no incremental predictive value versus each series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in RSAFS above normal retail-sales growth indicates accelerating household demand, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as analysts revise revenue expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds its prior 3-month average by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS acceleration events are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the sign of the effect flips in most rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises rapidly, the market should price weaker earnings and higher recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the labor market deterioration becomes visible.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average after UNRATE rises by 0.3pp over 3 months, or if the event win rate is below 40% across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and better cyclical earnings momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter when manufacturing activity is expanding.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum does not lead to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes negative in a majority of rolling 3-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad real demand growth across the economy and consumer sector, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter by improving revenue breadth and reducing recession risk.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1/RSAFS growth condition fails to outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day SPX returns, or if triggered periods do not show positive average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Low and falling CPIAUCSL combined with a near-zero FEDFUNDS rate signals disinflation with policy accommodation, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through a lower discount-rate channel.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend is falling while FEDFUNDS is below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the disinflation-plus-low-rates condition does not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if no incremental effect exists relative to CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A housing rebound in HOUST transmits to equity returns through construction, credit, and consumption channels, so rising housing starts should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the housing cycle turns up.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakout events do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is not above zero.
